title: mem0
oneLiner: A memory layer for AI applications and agents.
summary: mem0 is a memory layer for AI applications that helps agents store, retrieve, and reuse user and task context over time.
whyItMatters: Useful agents need continuity. A memory layer helps applications preserve context across sessions instead of treating every interaction as a blank slate.
notFor: Projects that cannot store user context
seoTitle: mem0: Open-source memory layer for AI agents
seoDescription: A concise editorial profile of mem0, including what it does, who it is best for, license, repo, and related open-source AI resources.
shareDescription: A memory layer for AI applications and agents.

coreStrengths.title: Persistent memory for AI apps
coreStrengths.description: mem0 is positioned as a memory layer that helps applications store and retrieve context across sessions.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Agents become more useful when they can remember user preferences, prior tasks, and long-running context instead of starting over.

coreStrengths.title: Context retrieval layer
coreStrengths.description: The project focuses on making relevant memories available to applications and agents when they need them.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Good retrieval is the difference between memory that helps and memory that only adds noise.

coreStrengths.title: Application-level personalization
coreStrengths.description: mem0 can support products that need user-specific or workflow-specific context over time.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: This is important for assistants, copilots, and internal tools that should adapt to recurring users.

useCaseNotes.title: Personalized AI assistants
useCaseNotes.description: Use it when an assistant needs to remember preferences, recurring entities, or prior conversations.

useCaseNotes.title: Agent task memory
useCaseNotes.description: A fit for workflows where an agent should reuse context from previous tasks instead of rebuilding it.

useCaseNotes.title: Context-aware product features
useCaseNotes.description: Useful for applications that need a memory layer without building every storage and retrieval pattern from scratch.

compareNotes.title: Choose it when memory is a product requirement
compareNotes.summary: Stateless chat is simpler, but mem0 becomes relevant when continuity and personalization are central to the experience.

compareNotes.title: Treat memory as sensitive infrastructure
compareNotes.summary: Before adopting a memory layer, teams should evaluate data retention, user control, and privacy requirements.

thumbnailBrief.visualMotif: layered memory cards and archive grid
thumbnailBrief.backgroundStyle: quiet editorial surface with stacked tiles
